body:
Dear Dr. Glass,

Thank you for submitting your manuscript "Diffuse CO2 and SO2 degassing along the Reykjanes Peninsula, 2024-2026" to the Journal of Volcanology and Geothermal Research. Both reviewers have now returned their reports and recommend minor revisions. The main requests concern the DOAS retrieval uncertainty treatment and a clearer comparison with the Fagradalsfjall 2021 baseline.

The full reviews are attached. We would appreciate a revised version within six weeks. Please let me know if you need any extension.

Kind regards,
Editorial Office, JVGR
snippet: Thank you for submitting your manuscript on Reykjanes degassing. Both reviewers ...
subject: JVGR-2026-0412: Decision - minor revisions

body:
Dear Editorial Office,

Thank you, this is very encouraging news. The reviewers' points on the DOAS retrieval uncertainty are fair and I can address them. I will expand the comparison with the 2021 Fagradalsfjall baseline as suggested.

Six weeks is comfortable; I expect to return the revised manuscript well before the deadline. My PhD student Lilja Einarsdóttir will help reprocess the flux time series.

Best regards,
Martha Glass
Icelandic Geophysical Institute
snippet: Thank you, this is very encouraging news. The reviewers' points on the DOAS retr...
subject: Re: JVGR-2026-0412: Decision - minor revisions

body:
Ciao Martha,

Quick coordination before Tuesday's partner call. The Etna MultiGAS station feed is now uploading to the EU-VGAS portal again after the firmware fix. Can you confirm the Reykjanes CO2/SO2 flux series for May-June is in as well? I'd like us both to walk the consortium through the cross-station comparison.

Also, for the Year 2 progress report to the EU, do you want to take the gas-geochemistry section and I take the instrumentation section? We should divide before the Brussels deadline.

A presto,
Marco
snippet: Quick coordination before Tuesday's partner call. The Etna MultiGAS station feed...
subject: EU-VGAS portal data + Year 2 report split

body:
Ciao Marco,

Reykjanes flux series for May and June is uploaded, Lilja finished the reprocessing yesterday, so the portal should show continuous coverage now. The cross-station comparison with Etna will be a strong slide for the consortium.

Dividing the report that way works well. I'll take gas geochemistry, you take instrumentation. Let's draft outlines this week and merge before the Brussels deadline. Talk Tuesday.

Best,
Martha
snippet: Reykjanes flux series for May and June is uploaded, Lilja finished the reprocess...
subject: Re: EU-VGAS portal data + Year 2 report split

body:
Hi Martha,

Following up on the Katla Readiness Assessment. I've finished a first pass at the jökulhlaup scenarios for the gas-geochemistry chapter, three drainage cases depending on where subglacial melting concentrates. The worst case puts a meltwater pulse down the Múlakvísl within hours.

Can we meet to align your degassing estimates with my flood timelines before you lock the chapter? The October 2 deadline is closer than it feels. I've put my draft figures in the shared Drive folder.

Best,
Helgi
snippet: Following up on the Katla Readiness Assessment. I've finished a first pass at th...
subject: Katla chapter - jökulhlaup scenarios for review

body:
Hi Helgi,

This is exactly what I needed. The Múlakvísl pulse timing matters for how I frame the pre-eruptive degassing signal, if we can show gas flux rising before the flood, that's a real warning-time argument for the civil-protection chapter.

Let's meet this week. I'll bring the Katla MultiGAS and DOAS estimates so we can overlay them on your flood timelines. Are you free Thursday afternoon at ÍJE, or would a video call be easier from your end?

Martha
snippet: This is exactly what I needed. The Mulakvisl pulse timing matters for how I fram...
subject: Re: Katla chapter - jökulhlaup scenarios for review

body:
Thursday at ÍJE works for me, say 14:00 in the Natural Hazards meeting room? I'll bring the revised drainage maps printed at A1 so we can mark up the gas-flux overlays directly.

One more thing: do you want to co-author the warning-time argument as a short standalone note? I think it's strong enough that we shouldn't bury it only inside the readiness report. We could submit it to the EGU session in spring.

See you Thursday,
Helgi
snippet: Thursday at ÍJE works for me, say 14:00 in the Natural Hazards meeting room? I'l...
subject: Re: Katla chapter - jökulhlaup scenarios for review

body:
14:00 Thursday in the Natural Hazards room is perfect. A1 prints will make the overlays much easier than squinting at a screen.

Yes to the standalone note, I agree it's too important to bury in the readiness report. The pre-eruptive gas-flux-before-flood angle deserves its own venue, and an EGU session next spring is a good target. Let's sketch an abstract once we've overlaid the data Thursday.

Thanks Helgi, looking forward to it.
Martha
snippet: 14:00 Thursday in the Natural Hazards room is perfect. A1 prints will make the o...
subject: Re: Katla chapter - jökulhlaup scenarios for review

body:
Hi everyone,

Locking in logistics for the July highland gas-monitoring campaign. Halldór has confirmed the Hekla Air Services helicopter for the Katla, Bárðarbunga, and Askja survey, weather permitting, with the field window 7-17 July.

Please confirm: (1) MultiGAS units serviced and calibrated, (2) drone sensors charged with spare batteries, (3) DOAS spectrometer packed, (4) personal cold-weather and safety gear. Lilja, can you own the instrument checklist? I'll handle permits and the ÍJE safety sign-off with Thorbjörg.

Martha
snippet: Locking in logistics for the July highland gas-monitoring campaign. Halldór has ...
subject: July highland campaign - logistics and checklist

body:
Hi Martha,

I'll own the instrument checklist. Both MultiGAS units are back from calibration and reading clean against the reference gas. Drone batteries are cycling now, I'll pack four spares since the highland cold drains them fast. DOAS spectrometer is serviced and the laptop has the latest retrieval software.

One question: do you want the spare CO2 sensor heads as backup? They add weight but Askja's plume was patchy last year and we lost a morning to a dead head.

Lilja
snippet: I'll own the instrument checklist. Both MultiGAS units are back from calibration...
subject: Re: July highland campaign - logistics and checklist

body:
Yes, bring the spare CO2 sensor heads, losing a morning at Askja last year cost us a full plume transect, and the weight is worth it. Good thinking.

I've submitted the highland access permits and Thorbjörg has signed off on the safety plan. Halldór wants final headcount and gear weight by 1 July so he can balance the load for the helicopter. Can you send me the packed-weight total once everything is boxed?

Thanks Lilja, this is shaping up well.
Martha
snippet: Yes, bring the spare CO2 sensor heads, losing a morning at Askja last year cost ...
subject: Re: July highland campaign - logistics and checklist

body:
Dear Dr. Glass,

This is a reminder regarding the European Volcanic Gas Network (EU-VGAS) Year 2 financial and scientific progress report. The combined report is due to the Commission by 15 September 2026.

Please ensure the gas-geochemistry deliverables and the data-portal usage statistics are included, along with the updated milestone table for the eight partner observatories. If you anticipate any deviation from the milestone schedule, flag it now so we can prepare the justification.

Regards,
EU-VGAS Grant Administration
snippet: This is a reminder regarding the EU-VGAS Year 2 financial and scientific progres...
subject: EU-VGAS Year 2 progress report - due 15 September

body:
Dear Grant Administration,

Noted, thank you. The gas-geochemistry deliverables are on track and I will provide the updated milestone table for all eight partner observatories. Marco Bianchi and I are dividing the scientific sections this week.

One point to flag: portal traffic and station feeds spiked during the recent Reykjanes unrest, which actually strengthens our public-engagement milestone, I'll include the usage statistics. I do not anticipate any deviation from schedule.

Best regards,
Martha Glass
snippet: Noted, thank you. The gas-geochemistry deliverables are on track and I will prov...
subject: Re: EU-VGAS Year 2 progress report - due 15 September

body:
Hey love,

Three quick family things before I forget. (1) Lily's school finishes for the summer next Friday, can you do the pickup? I have a dive survey out of the Oceanic Research Centre that morning. (2) Your dad called, he wants us all up in Akureyri for a weekend before the highland campaign. (3) We're low on Lily's lactose-free milk again.

Also don't forget you said you'd call Craig back about Oliver's birthday. Love you.

Derek
snippet: Three quick family things before I forget. Lily's school finishes for the summer...
subject: Family logistics this week

body:
I can do Friday pickup, no problem, my last seminar of term is in the morning but I'm clear by lunch. Yes to Akureyri; a weekend at Dad's before I disappear into the highlands sounds perfect. Let's aim for the weekend after next so Lily's done with school.

I'll grab the lactose-free milk on my way home tonight, and I'll call Craig about Oliver's birthday this evening, I keep meaning to and forgetting. Love you too.

Martha
snippet: I can do Friday pickup, no problem. My last seminar of term is in the morning bu...
subject: Re: Family logistics this week

body:
Perfect on all counts. I'll let your dad know we're coming the weekend after next, he'll be thrilled, he's already talking about doing a roast.

And thank you for grabbing the milk, you're a lifesaver. Lily will be glad. Drive safe, the Sæbraut was foggy this morning., Derek
snippet: Perfect on all counts. I'll let your dad know we're coming the weekend after nex...
subject: Re: Family logistics this week
